about.title: About Photo2URL
about.lead: Photo2URL is a free online photo to url converter — upload a PNG, JPG, WEBP, or GIF and get a photo to url link in one step. No sign-up, no ads, no file picker.
about.problemHeading: Why most image links come with friction
about.problemBody: Other services were built for page views, not link generation. They ask for an account before you upload. They wrap your image in a gallery page with ads and comments, hiding the direct URL behind menus. Unlike Imgur — which turns a simple upload into a full-page experience — Photo2URL is designed to convert photos to URLs, nothing more.
about.solutionHeading: How Photo2URL turns a photo into a link
about.solutionBody: Drag and drop, paste from clipboard, or click to browse. As soon as you upload a photo, the link generator returns three formats side by side: a direct image URL, a Markdown snippet, and an HTML embed. Copy the one you need — links work on Facebook, Twitter, Reddit, Discord, GitHub, and anywhere that accepts image URLs.
about.techBody: Behind the scenes, every image is stored on Cloudflare R2 and served through a global CDN over HTTPS. The free photo to url plan includes 10 uploads per day at 2 MB per file, stored for 30 days. Paid plans lift all limits and add permanent storage. The interface supports eight languages. Whether you need a PNG to URL converter for a README, a JPG to URL for a blog post, or a quick way to turn a screenshot into a shareable link, Photo2URL is built for that single task.

pricing.title: Simple Pricing
pricing.subtitle: Pay for what you need. Nothing you don't.
pricing.enterpriseHint: For teams that need their own image CDN and unlimited everything.

items.question: What is Photo To URL?
items.answer: Photo To URL is a free online tool that turns your photos into direct image links. You upload a JPG, PNG, WEBP, or GIF — we give you back a URL. No sign-up. No ads. The link works in documents, chat, forums, and anywhere you can paste text.

items.question: How do I convert a photo to a URL link?
items.answer: Drag and drop your photo, paste from clipboard, or click to browse. Photo To URL converts it instantly and returns a direct HTTPS link. The file keeps its original format — a .jpg stays a .jpg. No image processing, no format changes. The URL is ready to copy the moment the upload finishes.

items.question: Is the photo to URL converter free?
items.answer: Yes — and we mean it. The free tier includes 10 uploads per day, 2 MB per file, hosted for 30 days. No trial period. No credit card. Paid plans lift the limits and add permanent Cloudflare R2 storage, but the core converter stays free.

items.question: What kind of link do I get?
items.answer: You get a direct image URL — the link ends in .jpg, .png, .webp, or .gif. It's not a page with ads or embeds. You also get a Markdown snippet and an HTML img tag in the same result card. One upload, three formats. Copy the one you need.

items.question: Do the links work everywhere?
items.answer: Every link we generate is HTTPS and served from Cloudflare's global CDN. Paste it into a GitHub README, a Notion page, a Reddit comment, a Jira ticket, a Slack message — it renders the image directly. No embed code. No iframe. Just the image.

items.question: Can I use this for Facebook or social media?
items.answer: Yes. Upload your photo here, copy the direct URL, and paste it into a Facebook post, comment, or message. The link works the same way on Twitter, LinkedIn, Discord, and any other platform that supports image URLs. It's one link across every app.

items.question: What's the difference between Photo To URL and Imgur?
items.answer: Imgur wraps your image in a gallery page with ads and comments. Photo To URL gives you a raw image URL — no page, no ads, no clutter. Paste the link anywhere and the image shows up directly. For Markdown users, we also give you a ready-to-use snippet.

items.question: How do I cancel my subscription?
items.answer: Cancel recurring billing through your card issuer — mobile banking app, online banking, or customer service.

items.question: Can I get a refund?
items.answer: If the service was unavailable due to an issue on our side, contact support and we'll review your case. We aim to reply within 1–2 business days.

items.question: What happens after I cancel?
items.answer: You keep access for the period you already paid for. No further charges after that period ends.

brand.description: PhotoToURL is a free image to URL converter. Upload JPG, PNG, WEBP to get direct image URLs for sharing in docs, chat, tickets, and forums. Free tier: 10 uploads/day, 2 MB per file. Paid plans with CDN links on Cloudflare R2.

language.chooseLang: Choose your preferred language and region

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.content: We collect minimal data to provide our service. Uploaded files are stored securely on Cloudflare R2. We do not sell or share your personal data with third parties. For free users, files are automatically removed after 30 days. Paid users retain their files until they cancel their subscription. We use cookies for authentication and security purposes. By using this service, you agree to this privacy policy.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.content: By using photo2url, you agree not to upload illegal, harmful, or copyrighted content without permission. We reserve the right to remove any content that violates these terms. The service is provided 'as is' without warranty. We are not liable for any damages resulting from the use of our service. Free tier users are subject to daily upload limits and automatic file cleanup after 30 days. Paid plans are governed by the terms of our payment provider, Lemon Squeezy.

refund.title: Refund Policy
refund.content: If you are not satisfied with your paid subscription, you may request a refund within 7 days of purchase. Refunds are processed through Lemon Squeezy, our payment provider. To request a refund, please contact support at support@photo2url.com with your order details. After the 7-day window, subscriptions are non-refundable. Canceling a subscription stops future charges but does not refund the current billing period.
